label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 5

extraction.one_line_summary: SOLAIRE Marc attacked solo late on final Viveiro climb ascent, held off 6-second gap as chasing group stared each other down on descent
tactical_patterns: 50kph opening 45min tailwind pace fractured peloton's control mechanism, enabling 18-rider elite breakaway quality instead of usual fodder.
Wright/Mollard neutralized each other's red jersey chase in final 10km, neither willing to tow rival, gifting Solaire decisive margins.
Groupama-FDJ placed two riders (Mollard/Stewart) in 18-strong break, demonstrating coordinated team infiltration for jersey defense splitting duties.

rider_signals.SOLAIRE Marc: positive: bridged twice after initially struggling on first Viveiro passage, showing unusual resilience recovery for typical domestique role
rider_signals.STEWART Jake: negative: cramped late despite being in winning break position, suggesting insufficient preparation for 187km breakaway effort
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: neutral: refused final kilometer chase cooperation despite needing time gap, prioritizing sprint positioning over jersey mathematics
rider_signals.MOLLARD Rudy: positive: captured red jersey by tracing Wright's wheel perfectly while teammate Stewart sacrificed himself bridging earlier
